plot_frame_box.description: Plot frame detected via long dark axis lines: col 62 (left), col 685 (right), row 37 (top), row 392 (bottom). The two columns at 62/685 each have ~356 dark pixels (vertical spines); rows 37/392 each have ~630 dark pixels (horizontal axes).

data_to_pixel_formula.col: col = (x_value - b_x) / m_x  with m_x=0.016678, b_x=-1.223823
data_to_pixel_formula.row: row = (y_value - b_y) / m_y  with m_y=-0.028442, b_y=11.072470

worked_example.scenario: Detected curve-crossing point should land near image-frame center.
worked_example.verification: Visual: the two same-color curves cross at roughly the chart center, slightly right of x=6 and at y just under 5; predicted pixel sits inside the visible crossing region.

legend_exclusion_used_for_frame.description: Upper-left legend box containing two horizontal blue swatches plus 'Series A (rising)' and 'Series B (falling)' text. Masked out before tracing so the swatches don't pollute the trace seeds.

detection_internals.frame_rule: argmax dark-pixel-count rows and columns from cv2.cvtColor(BGR2GRAY) thresholded at gray<100.
detection_internals.tick_label_rule: Group contiguous dark columns/rows in the margin band (y-band cols 30..60; x-band rows 395..413) into label centers; reject the leftmost detection in the x-band (col 48) because it coincides with the y-axis title band tail.
